summary: Taxpayers can rely on Section 80C of the Income-Tax Act to reduce their tax liability through deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakh in a financial year. Here are the investment options you can choose from:

title: 2008 housing crash fears resurfaces — but can buyers really follow Dave Ramsey’s 25% mortgage rule in 2026?
summary: Concerns about a housing market crash are growing among Americans as mortgage rates rise and inflation increases. Financial experts recommend keeping housing costs below 25% of income, though this is becoming difficult with rising home prices. However, a major crash like in 2008 seems unlikely.

title: DA: Highest Dearness Allowance increase of 11% was in 2021 in the 7th CPC — Here's how much hikes have been announced
summary: DA hike: The highest hike for Dearness Allowance of central government employees came in 2021 with an 11% increase from the central pay commission. Here's a look at what increments have been announced over the past few years…

title: 8th CPC: What is the role of pay commissions in calculation of Dearness Allowance?
summary: 8th central pay commission: The government appointed panel determines Dearness Allowance hike, fitment factor and other allowances for central govt employees. Here's what role it plays in the decision process…

title: Bank deposit risk explained: What happens to your money if a lender fails to return it?
summary: Bank deposits are generally safe, but in case of bank failure, only ₹5 lakh per depositor is insured under the DICGC. This coverage applies to all accounts in a bank, with specific exclusions for certain deposits. Details here.&nbsp;

title: Tax-saving FDs vs regular FDs: Interest rates, lock-in and tax benefits compared
summary: Fixed deposits (FDs) are favored for stable returns, with banks in India offering rates up to 7%. Tax-saving FDs provide deductions under Section 80C but have a 5-year lock-in. Interest rates vary, with senior citizens receiving higher rates.

title: Sovereign Gold Bonds: RBI announces redemption price for 2018 series payable on 4 May; here's how much you gain
summary: The RBI has set the final redemption price for its Sovereign Gold Bond 2018-19 Series-I at ₹14,901 per unit, payable on 4 May 2026. Investors could see gains of nearly 386% from the issue price of ₹3,114 per unit.

title: Income tax: How much cash can you deposit to prepay home loan principal without attracting I-T notice?
summary: Income tax rules discourage cash transactions, limiting annual deposits to ₹10 lakh. Experts recommend using savings accounts for cash deposits

title: How to claim stuck NPS funds with interest? Step-by-step guide for subscribers
summary: Unclaimed National Pension System contributions can be retrieved through a mechanism specified by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority. Full details here.

title: Family floater vs separate policy: What’s best health insurance policy for your elderly parents?
summary: Family floater health insurance premiums are primarily calculated based on the age of the eldest member covered. Other key factors are - sum insured, medical history of family members, and family size&nbsp;

title: CDSL Q4 Results: Cons PAT declines 20% to Rs 80 crore, Rs 12.75/share dividend declared
summary: CDSL Q4 Results: Central Depository Services Limited reported a mixed Q4 FY26 performance, with revenue growing 17.1% to Rs 262.85 crore, but net profit declining 20% to Rs 80.22 crore. title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Results: PAT jumps 13% to Rs 4,026.55 crore, NII rises 8%
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Results: Kotak Mahindra Bank on Saturday reported a net profit of Rs 4,026.55 crore for the March quarter of FY26, marking a jump of 13.3% from Rs 3,552 crore posted in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year.

title: Nifty stays range bound as volatility rises; breakout awaited
summary: Nifty 50 traded within a narrow range, closing slightly higher amid rising volatility and weak momentum indicators. Markets remain in consolidation, with key resistance and support levels intact, while sector rotation suggests selective opportunities despite the absence of strong directional trends.

title: AI spending boom soars but no returns for big tech giants, warns Jefferies’ Chris Wood
summary: Rising AI investments by global tech giants are straining cash flows, with capex nearing record levels relative to operating cash flow. title: Largecap IT stocks as a value play? BNP Paribas’ Kumar Rakesh issues a reality check
summary: BNP Paribas analyst Kumar Rakesh cautions that weak FY27 guidance, AI-led disruption, and stalled client spending challenge the IT sector’s “value” narrative. While buybacks and dividends provide support, earnings risks remain elevated, making selective stock picking crucial for investors.
